source.multipart_basis: The original sample already specifies body_count=assembly with 'rock + wire cage + anchor pins as sub-bodies'. Multipart decomposition formalizes those three sub-bodies plus the upstream/downstream aprons that prevent scour.

full_prompt:
Design the ASABE CN13 Gunlogson farm check-dam structure for edge-of-field stormwater sediment capture as a multi-part assembly, explicitly modeling the 2.4 m wide x 0.90 m long x 0.45 m tall rock-filled gabion check-dam as the physical collection of the riprap rock fill, the galvanized hardware-cloth gabion cage, steel anchor pins, and upstream/downstream scour aprons. The original prompt already calls out 'deliver STEP AP242 assembly of the gabion check-dam with rock + wire cage + anchor pins as sub-bodies'; this multipart sidecar formalizes that decomposition and adds the apron families.

The assembly consists of four part families: (A) riprap rock fill, angular stone D50 150 mm filling the gabion volume (2.4 x 0.90 x 0.45 m = 0.972 m^3 of rock); (B) the galvanized hardware-cloth gabion cage, a 2.7 mm galvanized-wire frame forming the 2.4 m wide x 0.90 m long x 0.45 m tall cage around the rock fill with wire tie closures; (C) ASTM A615 Grade 60 16 mm-diameter anchor pins driven into the waterway bottom to tie the gabion to the soil and resist sliding; (D) upstream and downstream scour aprons, two flat layers of riprap or geotextile + riprap that extend 0.60 m upstream and 1.20 m downstream of the dam to prevent undermining.

Geometry inherits the original sample verbatim: dam 2.4 m wide (perpendicular to flow) x 0.90 m long (along flow) x 0.45 m tall, D50 riprap 150 mm, grassed waterway 2% longitudinal slope.

Material inherits verbatim: riprap = angular stone D50 150 mm; gabion wire = galvanized hardware cloth, 2.7 mm wire; anchor pins = ASTM A615 Grade 60, 16 mm diameter.

Loads inherit verbatim: LC1 10-year peak discharge 0.85 m^3/s steady-state weir flow over the dam; LC2 sediment + debris static hydrostatic + accumulated sediment thrust.

Pass/fail criteria inherit verbatim from the original and apply at the assembly level: (R1) ponded stage behind dam under 0.85 m^3/s <= 0.40 m (grassed waterway bank freeboard); (R2) sediment trapping efficiency over the 10-year storm >= 60% (USDA SEDIMOT Stokes settling); (R3) sliding FoS of gabion against hydrostatic thrust + debris >= 1.5; (R4) overturning FoS about downstream toe >= 2.0; (R5) gabion wire pull-out FoS against tensile tearing of galvanized wire under sediment + debris >= 2.0. Added assembly-level targets under fem_check_policy.assembled_only: anchor-pin pullout DCR and apron-to-gabion interface continuity must be reported; per-part stresses are reported for diagnostic purposes but do not gate pass/fail.

Vibration, thermal, fluid CFD, radiation, and buckling analyses are not required; hydraulic load is applied via broad-crested weir equations plus closed-form Stokes settling. Deliver STEP AP242 of the assembly with each body (rock fill volume, wire cage, each anchor pin, upstream apron, downstream apron) as a distinct solid tagged with part_family (A..D) and instance_index, plus a hydrology and stability report with ponded stage, sediment trapping efficiency, sliding FoS, overturning FoS, wire pull-out FoS, assembly mass, and anchor-pin + apron DCR.
short_prompt:
Design the ASABE CN13 Gunlogson farm check-dam as a multi-part gabion assembly (riprap rock fill, galvanized hardware-cloth cage, 16 mm anchor pins, upstream + downstream scour aprons).

Dam 2.4 m wide x 0.90 m long x 0.45 m tall in a 2% grassed waterway; riprap D50 150 mm filling 0.972 m3; cage 2.7 mm galvanized wire; anchor pins ASTM A615 Grade 60 16 mm diameter; upstream apron 0.60 m, downstream apron 1.20 m.

Load cases: LC1 10-year peak discharge 0.85 m3/s over the dam; LC2 sediment + debris static hydrostatic + accumulated sediment thrust.

Pass/fail: ponded stage <= 0.40 m; sediment trapping efficiency >= 60% (USDA SEDIMOT Stokes); sliding FoS >= 1.5; overturning FoS >= 2.0; wire pullout FoS >= 2.0; FEM at assembled level; anchor + apron DCR <= 1.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Peak stage and discharge-over-dam are closed-form weir equations (Henderson 1966), not FEA or CFD.
Sediment trapping efficiency is computed by particle settling velocity over residence time per USDA SEDIMOT.

prompt.title: Gabion check-dam for farm stormwater sediment capture (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part gabion check-dam: riprap rock fill (A) + galvanized wire cage (B) + 16 mm anchor pins (C) + upstream/downstream scour aprons (D). 2.4 m wide x 0.90 m long x 0.45 m tall in 2% grassed waterway. 10-year 0.85 m^3/s event with >= 60% sediment trapping and stability FoS >= 1.5/2.0/2.0.

parts.name: riprap_rock_fill
parts.role: angular stone D50 150 mm filling the gabion volume; provides mass and roughness for hydraulic energy dissipation and the bulk sliding/overturning resistance
parts.count_note: single filled volume; may be modeled as bulk-density solid for STEP + limit-equilibrium analysis

parts.name: galvanized_wire_cage
parts.role: 2.7 mm galvanized hardware-cloth cage forming the gabion frame; confines the rock fill and carries tensile membrane stress under hydrostatic + debris loading

parts.name: anchor_pin
parts.role: ASTM A615 Grade 60 16 mm rebar pin driven into the waterway bottom at the gabion corners; ties the cage to the soil and provides sliding resistance at the base
parts.count_note: typical detail uses 4-8 corner pins; minimum 4 corners

parts.name: scour_apron
parts.role: upstream and downstream scour apron (flat riprap or geotextile + riprap) extending 0.60 m upstream and 1.20 m downstream of the dam; prevents undermining under the 0.85 m^3/s weir flow

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 2.4 m wide x (0.60 + 0.90 + 1.20) = 2.70 m along flow x 0.45 m tall
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: waterway coordinates: +x along flow direction, +y across waterway, +z up from bottom

boundary_conditions.dam_base: set in waterway bottom, passive soil resistance on downstream toe

objective.primary: pass ponded stage + trapping efficiency + stability under assembled_only FEM check

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: galvanized wire ultimate vs cage tensile demand
anchor-pin pullout capacity per NAVFAC DM 7.02 + apron-to-gabion continuity under weir flow
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

interfaces.load_path: rock fill confined inside the wire cage via bearing contact; transfers hoop/lateral confining stress from rock to wire
wire cage tied to each corner anchor pin via wire loops; transfers sliding/uplift reaction into the driven pin
gabion face overlaps with the upstream/downstream apron riprap; prevents undermining at the toe
apron anchored at corners with additional rebar pins to resist flood uplift

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Gabion check-dams are physically rock fill confined by a galvanized wire cage with driven anchor pins plus scour aprons; the multipart sidecar formalizes that as four part families.
notes.speculation_caveats: Apron dimensions and anchor-pin count are inferred from typical farm check-dam details; the original sample only calls out rock + wire cage + pins without apron dimensions.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
